name: INR/USD Exchange Rate
summary: Indian rupee at record lows. Hit ₹93.797/USD on Mar 20. RBI sold $15B+ in March to defend. Goldman Sachs forecast ₹95. Driven by oil import bill ballooning, FII outflows, and dollar strength.

current.snapshot_note: yfinance Mar 28; RBI close Mar 27 at 94.81. ₹95 trigger 20 paisa away.

price_snapshots.delta_context: Rupee was already under pressure from FII outflows in 2025

price_snapshots.trigger: Record low on Iraq force majeure + oil spike
price_snapshots.delta_context: Goldman forecast ₹95 if oil stays elevated

signals.content: Rupee breached ₹92/USD for first time. RBI sold $18-20B in single week. Foreign exchange reserves declined $11.68B by Mar 7.
Rupee hit record ₹93.797/USD. Goldman Sachs predicted ₹95 amid oil surge. BusinessToday asked: 'Is the rupee headed for 100 mark?'

Rupee weakened to fresh record 93.89/USD in early trade despite Trump de-escalation. India's markets fell with Asia (traded before Trump announcement). RBI continues heavy intervention but reserves declining.

Rupee hit intraday low of 94.85/USD on March 27, closed at 94.81. Down 4%+ since war began. RBI intervening with net short dollar position approaching $100B. Forex reserves dropped $7B to $709.75B. Approaching psychological 95 level.

edges.context: Rupee depreciation increases import costs, widens CAD, triggers FII outflows, and makes Indian assets less attractive to foreign investors.
Higher oil → more dollar demand for imports → rupee weakens. Each $10/bbl rise adds pressure.
RBI's primary focus. Selling dollars, buying rupee bonds. Strategy: 'calibrated, smooth depreciation' not reversal.
Weak rupee → FIIs sell to avoid currency losses → equity selling pressure. ₹52,704 crore FII outflow in March.

trigger_points.mechanism: Would signal loss of RBI control. Emergency measures possible. FII exodus would accelerate dramatically.
